title: Merxmuellera arundinacea
taxonomy: Merxmuellera arundinacea is a genus of grasses, and it is classified within the family Poaceae. This classification places it within the monocots, a group of flowering plants with a single cotyledon in their seeds. The Poaceae family is a large and diverse group of grasses, and Merxmuellera arundinacea is one of the many species within it. The genus Merxmuellera is often placed within the subfamily Danthonioideae, which is a group of grasses native to southern Africa. This classification helps to group Merxmuellera arundinacea with other similar species and understand its evolutionary relationships.

intro: Merxmuellera arundinacea, also known as Mountain Rest, is a perennial grass native to Australia, characterized by its slender stems and panicles of yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and coughs, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Merxmuellera arundinacea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or a tincture, and is also used to make a smoke infusion, which is inhaled to relieve respiratory symptoms.
